It had a wide range of new-release movies, documentaries, music, and games. The touch screen was responsive, and the interface was easy to navigate. Onboard Wi-Fi was available and worked well for light browsing and messaging. The cabin crew on QR039 were attentive and professional. From helping with bags to offering water before being asked, their service was personal and polished. The flight departed on time and landed at Paris Charles de Gaulle smoothly. Disembarkation was quick, and baggage arrived without delay, rounding off a flawless travel experience.

Review Title: service was both warm and precise
Review: Copenhagen to Doha on Qatar Airways Business Class (B787-8). This flight did not feature the Qsuite layout, but the standard Business Class seating was extremely comfortable. Qatar’s cabin crew delivered service that was both warm and precise, among the best in the industry. Overall, the experience reflected a high standard. There were, however, a few shortcomings. The pilot’s announcement was difficult to understand, marred by poor audio quality. The WiFi connection was sluggish and unreliable, despite the $20 charge. As for the food, I chose the Indian vegetarian option, which arrived swimming in oil. A lighter, more balanced preparation would have been preferable.

Review Title: maintains a high standard of service
Review: Doha to Goa on Qatar Airways Business Class (B787-8). This flight also lacked the Qsuite configuration, but the conventional Business Class seating was more than comfortable for a short 3-hour hop. Qatar maintains a high standard of service, and the crew lived up to that reputation. The flight left Doha and reached Goa on schedule. The only letdown was the WiFi. I paid the full $20 and once again received only patchy service. One hopes this will be addressed once Starlink is introduced across the fleet.

On 23 June 2025, I flew from Hong Kong to Doha. The flight was diverted and arrived in Doha later than scheduled due to the Iranian attack on Qatar, and this caused a substantial delay for my trip. When I arrived in Doha, I learned that the connecting flight to Sao Paulo on 24th, QR779, was canceled.  I approached staff at the airport but he was not at all helpful. The only thing he could tell me was to go to the transfer counter. When I went to the transfer counter area, I witnessed pandemonium. I could not figure out how to line up for the transfer desk, and asked another staff and he told me to go to the business class lounge. I followed that advice and went there. A staff there told me to just wait for a few hours and come back because the company is handling the situation and things should go back to normal then. In the end, I had to spend 20 some hours before I can get a new ticket, and another 20-some hours at the airport before getting on the plane to Sao Paulo on 26th, two days later than the original connection flight. Meanwhile Qatar Airways put my luggage to an earlier flight to Sao Paulo, I had a tagging device to learn that my luggage was already in Sao Paulo for some time when I arrived there.
Route: Hong Kong to Sao Paulo via Doha

Review: Boarding at Doha airport was chaotic and unorganised, despite gate being manned by 6 agents. When lines started to form at boarding time, the supervisor screamed at the guests: "Sit down, the aircraft is still being cleaned; you need to be patient." Chaos followed when boarding finally started 35 min late. Economy started first which caused business class passengers to complain, which resulted in a rather messy boarding procedure. The service and food was the big letdown on this 6 hour night flight. Service completed almost 3 hours after takeoff which left 2 hours of rest before the lights went on again. The meal I received was a soggy stick mass of pasta with of tomato sauce. (They called it Penne Arabiata) There was no taste and an unpleasant texture. This was one of the worst inflight meal I ever had. The flight attendant in charge of my section was slow, unmotivated and robotic in her service rendered. The seats on the B789 were tight and uncomfortable, making it impossible for a good rest.

Qatar Airways cancelled the connecting flight on the departure date when I was at the airport to check in for the first flight. So I had to quickly get another flight with another airline at the airport to fly on the same day. They did not contact me to inform that they can only process a refund if I cancel the return flight as well, which I only found out a couple days after I contacted the airline to check on the partial refund for cancelling my departure flights.

Review Title: a good airline and decent value
Review: First trip using Qatar Airways. My lasting impression is that the cabin crew work extremely hard throughout the whole flights, unlike the crew on certain other airlines. The food in Economy was very poor though. The safety briefings are via pre-recorded videos featuring an actor who turns the whole issue of safety into a huge joke, which is really inappropriate. The pilots were superb and made incredibly smooth landings on all flights. Overall a good airline and decent value. That food though..oh dear!
Route: Bangkok to London Heathrow via Doha

Review: Flight from Bali to Doha arrived 8 mins early, flight from Doha to Vienna arrived 77 minutes late. 2 toilets for 130 passengers in economy on the A320. Although Wheel chair was requested I had to walk to the aircraft.

Review Title: pay attention to details that matter
Review: It is always a pleasure to experience Qatar's world class service. They are the best airline because they pay attention to details that matter to the customer. The cabin crew is efficient professionals. I like that they always know what they are doing, as if they take pride in what they are doing. They are also very warm and personable.  They chat with you rather than talk to you - if you understand what I mean. I also like that they remember your preferences OR take initiative when they need to. My cabin attendant remembered that I preferred sparkling water and delivered it with each meal, but he also knew with which of my meal courses to serve my two different wine selections. The food is excellent and the restrooms are kept clean. Pilots y communicate ahead of time if there will be turbulence or if any information needs to be shared. Little things like these make me feel like they care and if they care then I feel comfortable and safe.

Al Safwa lounge in Doha outstanding as always. Last minute aircraft switch from A350-1000 to B773, but was equipped with Qsuite. Holding area at Gate A7 did not provide seats for the fully booked flight. Flight departed 30 min later, which resulted in a 1h delay into Heathrow, because of the restricted airspace at the moment. Food was great. Caviar service was a nice touch. The service was ok, but lacked in consistency. Coffee was served 30 min before the meal arrived, and it also took up to 30 min to clear plate between courses during the meal. New WiFi lighting fast, which resulted in loud WiFi chats among some pax. I had to tell them to stop! I think this will become a big issue in the future and IFA should be firmer on those inconsiderate people. Overall a great flight with some room for improvement for onboard service

Check in at Sydney was quick efficient and very friendly staff. Boarding a little chaotic, it was a free for all and anyone used any Boarding lane. Eventually on board, very friendly and personalised greeting. Not long after settling in, pretake off drink offered, explanation of seat operation, menu and how dining on demand works. Not long after, meal orders were taken. Once airborne, the crew switched into their routine. It was faultless. It was consistent and again personable. The seating was comfortable. In lie flat position, I slept a solid 8 hours. All food was of a high standard, well presented and taste was fantastic. Beverages were also well presented and always available. The highlight for the wife and to a lesser extent me, was the inflight bar and lounge which was well patronised when we were there. The crew also engaging and very friendly and genuinely interested in their guests. Overall, we rate this flight tops. Way above some of the other major airlines we have flown and better than First Class on some... just saying. Consistency, personable and a very enjoyable first time experience flying with Qatar Airways

Review Title: Service hectic and not overly friendly
Review: Check in fast and friendly. Contract lounge with poor food selection and uncleared tables. B773 on this 4h red eye night flight. Boarding via remote stand. I was the first on board, but was ignored by the FA and had to ask for my welcome drink while the plane was already moving. Meal order was taken after take off, which isn’t ideal for a red eye flight. The new irritating safety video was shown with no sound. Service was hectic and not overly friendly. An orange light was left on throughout the night. Overall, a rather average flight. The same flight from Doha to ADD was in comparison so much better.

Al Safwa lounge great as always for my long layover. Boarding via gate rather unorganised. A320 with the flat bed seats. Food and drinks choices outstanding for the 4h flight. What made the flight special was the service rendered by the hardworking cabin crew. It’s not easy to provide a full service for an almost full cabin on a narrow body aircraft, but the two in the front cabin worked wonders; highly recommended!

Check in was friendly and efficient. Contract Miracle lounge at the new Satellite terminal quiet, with poor breakfast options. I should have used the other OW lounges like Cathay or JAL. It’s hard to comprehend why Qatar does not give access to their own lounge to QR Platinum members.  Flight was uneventful with average service and food. WiFi did not work most of the time, seats tight and hard. Flight arrived 15 minutes ahead of schedule

Review Title: they declined a small goodwill gesture
Review: There were several issues, including the onboard service, condition of the lavatory, problems with the moving map and a disappointing breakfas. I contacted Customer Care, and while they responded promptly, I was disappointed that they declined even a small goodwill gesture.

Two and a half hours before our flight to Indonesia, we arrived at check-in and an unexpected Advance Passenger Processing (APP) flag locked my visa. My wife and son were cleared to board, but I was blocked. The initial supervisor emailed immigration authorities, informing us that waiting was our only option. I pleaded with the shift supervisor. He calmly replied, "Unfortunately, an email is the only way, but we will do everything we can to make sure you don't miss this flight." He kept his word, monitoring communications while front desk assistant fired off multiple "Urgent" follow-up emails in under ten minutes, pushing for a rapid response. Staff showed incredible leadership. He took decisive action to manually navigate the sync delay, forcefully pushing our check-in through.  We had 25 minutes to clear immigration, pass security, and reach Gate 24C. We reached the gate at 6:40 PM, ten minutes past takeoff. The gate agent had held the flight.

Came in on a connecting flight on Cathay Pacific flight from Guangzhou going onto Doha. At 2 AM everything at the airport was closed except for the lounges. Onboard nice QSuites but I just had to just sleep, and had no food or used entertainment.
A330 comfortable but very much underwhelming. Paper bags with a sandwich no choice, but as the flight that lasts 45 minutes getting there was more important.

Review Title: cabin crew was professional and friendly
Review: I recently flew with Qatar Airways on a Boeing 777-300ER and my experience was mixed overall, so I would rate it 3 stars. The onboard entertainment system was excellent, with a great selection of movies and internet access throughout the flight. The meals and drinks were also very good, and the onboard service from the cabin crew was professional and friendly. However, my seats was quite small and narrow. One of the biggest disappointments was the cabin baggage allowance of only 7kg, andthe extra baggage charges were 276 Chinese RMB (40usd- 35euros) per additional kilogram. The ground staff experience in Guangzhou was very unpleasant and poorly handled

Outstanding experience with Qatar Airways My recent journey was particularly memorable, as it was my second experience flying business class and it exceeded expectations.  Priority check-in eliminated long queues, and lounge access provided a calm, comfortable environment to relax. Onboard, the seat was spacious and converted into a fully flat bed. The service was attentive without being intrusive, and the dining experience stood out as well, thoughtfully presented, with a variety of options. A special mention goes to the cabin crew whose service truly elevated the journey. Highly recommended.

Very nice QSuites, I had 5A which is back facing. Being tired I was not interested in having any food. The WIFI was great throughout the flight except over India. Cabin staff were nice not top notch Qatar Airways standard but still very good.

Frankfurt Airport check in was chaotic, it took me over an hour to check in. Flight to Doha was ok, with decent food and good service. Arrival transport in Doha was by remote stand and bus, despite a quiet airport. Al Safwa lounge good as always. Departure was by remote stand and bus and it wasn’t clear when BC was suppose to board as the special bus for BC wasn’t there. Going through this at 3 am contributes to the stress of travelling. Food was ok on the 2nd leg of the journey, service was very good

Evening departure from London and early morning arriving into Doha the timing didn’t give an opportunity fee to enjoy meals or entertainment but the service was of excellent quality. WIFI was excellent and the flight was short for the route the unfortunate issue of having remote parking in Doha can be a problem.

Boarding in Johannesburg was chaotic there should have been two airbridges but only one was utilized and this was on the ground staff in JNB. There was a menu but what was on the menu wasn’t what was catered and I ended up having to settle for a cheese platter of the lowest quality. I think Qatar Airways need to review their ground operations and catering in Johannesburg as I have done this route three times a couple of years ago and had a wonderful experience.

My first experience on the Qatar Airways new Boeing 787-9 and they are equipped with a Qsuite while the 787-8 still has the old product. Wonderful IFE though it did not have Starlink WiFi just the old system. Very comfortable flight.

My flight from Bangkok to Prague on 15 March was canceled due to the Middle East conflict 3 days before departure via email. No alternative was provided other than a flight a week later. ​Zero Communication: I sent 6 emails, tried the hotline repeatedly, used Live Chat, and reached out on X but absolute silence. No offer of accommodation, meals, no realistic re-routing options. I was forced to buy a new ticket with China Airlines at my own expense to get home

By far the cheapest option on short notice between Amsterdam and Bangkok. Paid little over 800 euros for a return. which was a very good price. Got the chance of an upgrade to business class however for a good price and decided to treat myself. From the moment you step on board you feel welcome. Adressed by your name and the stewardesses introduce themselves. Welcome drinks, dine on demand, great food, a great lie-flat seat and the cabin almost felt homely. Second leg equally good. I love the lounge in the A380. Relaxed boarding and out in no time.  Unfortunately my return flight was cancelled 4 times due to the conflict in the Middle East. I must say that I saw a lot of complaints about QR in which the airline was accused of not responding or not being reachable. My experience is different. I had to call them 4 times and I must I got through pretty quick. Yes, off course there is a waiting time in such an extreme situation. They easily booked me on a new flight every time it was cancelled. Since I could work from Thailand, I was not in a big hurry to get a flight home. After the last cancellation I requested to book me on another airline. Which was granted promptly.

Check in chaos and bag drop took over one hour mostly due to extensive documentation checks by staff and oversized baggage. Boarding chaos and staff dealing with people in raised tone to control the volatile situation. Food main was tasty if the whole tray was rather small and main was cold (but tasty and edible). Cabin groomed to a high standard as were the staff.  I ordered a beer and it didn’t appear until a polite reminder 20 minutes later. Ticket was cheap so no real complaints. Would try again and hope for an improvement in check in and service levels.

Review Title: experience was not one of our best
Review: My wife and I have flown Qatar many times, but this experience was not one of our best. Hard product was good, although the 777 used on the DOH-MLE-DOH sector must be one of the oldest in their fleet and feels it. Soft product was not up to scratch on the LGW-DOH leg. This was a holiday flight and my wife and I like our wine. The brut champagne ran out before we were an hour into the flight, and the sauvignon blanc ran out before we were half way through the six hour flight.

Bought a Business Class Lite ticket with no lounge access. First flight in QSuites was fine. In Doha our plane to Vie was 4 hours late due to operational reasons. No compensation offered, nothing. Flight itself was ok with a very mediocre lunch.

Parts of the seat in business class were broken and I wouldn't be able to use the arm rest. They changed my pre arranged seat to another, and when I asked why the ground staff said we can change your seat.

Goa (Mopa) to Doha in Business Class on a Boeing 787-8. The short three-hour flight departed on time. The cabin featured Qatar Airways’ older Business Class configuration rather than the Qsuite, but the seat remained very comfortable. On-board service was efficient and unobtrusive. Overall, a predictably high-quality product from this airline.

Doha to Copenhagen in Business Class on a Boeing 787-9. The aircraft, about 6 years old, carried Qatar Airways’ pre-Qsuite Business Class cabin. In theory, still a premium product, but my seat was cramped and uncomfortable. The stewardess was disengaged, showed no warmth, and operated on autopilot, as if merely ticking boxes rather than attending to passengers. This was an unusually poor Qatar Airways Business Class experience.

It was a fantastic experience from start to finish. The crew was professional, friendly, and helpful. I want to give a special mention to crew member named Denise whose service was outstanding. Additionally, the cabin manager was engaging and ensured everything ran smoothly. I am very impressed with the service and look forward to flying with Qatar Airways again.

Check in at Frankfurt Terminal 2 fast and friendly. Contract lounge average with poor F/B choices. Boarding not well organized despite having many gate agents on duty. I was asked to take a seat in the separate business class section of the waiting area. The section was already occupied by economy class pax since there was nobody to check the boarding passes at the entrance to the section. The flight itself was uneventful with decent food and drinks with excellent service provided by the chief purser of the flight being in charge of my side of the cabin. He worked tirelessly throughout the night and even explained the different wines; something which I never experienced after 100+ flights on Qatar. It’s unfortunate that I can’t recall his name, but I remember that he was from Malaysia. Arrival in Doha was unfortunately at another bus gate, despite having so many available contact gates. This cut my visit at the Al Safwa lounge short and there was just time to have a quick coffee. The flight to BKK on a full A380 was uneventful with again great service provided. Food on this flight was average. WiFi did not work throughout the flight

A complete lack of communication when trying to get in touch about baggage that was lost en route. The Chat on WhatsApp hasn't been answered in over 30 hours and the email I sent, not responded to so far. I called customer service who offered no help other than telling me to go to the website and couldn't offer any useful answer. On the flight the service was inconsistent. Twice, the food choices ran out offering only 2 meals on a duration of 17 hours. The in flight entertainment options were good but the seats were on the small side.

From the moment I stepped aboard my Qatar Airways flight in Djibouti on January 8, 2026, until arrival Milan Malpensa the following day, I was enveloped in a world of elegance, comfort, and hospitality.  The cabin crew on both legs of my journey Djibouti to Doha, and Doha to Milan were nothing short of extraordinary. Their warmth, professionalism, and genuine attentiveness elevated every moment onboard.  Before I could even glance toward my empty glass, a smiling attendant was already offering a refill.  The cuisine was restaurant-quality and paired with premium beverages. Even the snacks between meals were memorable. And when I mentioned how much I enjoyed the chips, they brought me a second chips with a smile.  My stopover in Hamad Airport was magical and transformed my layover into a luxurious interlude. The seamless transit, spotless facilities, and intuitive design made navigating the airport effortless and even enjoyable.
Route: Djibouti to Milan via Doha Hamad

Review: Check in, security check and immigration at BKK airport efficient and friendly. Contract lounge at the new Satellite Terminal average. There was an aircraft change from an ex CX aircraft with their premium economy seats, which I booked to an A380. Seat on the small economy section upstairs was quiet and reasonably comfortable. WiFi did not work. Catering was average with no butter, cheese or crackers. Main meal was ok. Service was fast and friendly. Boarding of the last sector at Doha airport was disorganized. After being toldat that the flight was not ready to board at boarding time and to take a seat, boarding started a minute later. Unfortunately, that was just half the case because the aircraft wasn’t ready and everyone had to wait on the airbridge for 20 minutes. The flight was uneventful with the same catering issues as the previous flight. WiFi was patchy, but worked at times. Flight arrived 20 min ahead of schedule. Service was somehow slow on this red eye flight.

As a business class traveller the service with Qatar is excellent. The check in at Heathrow seamless and the lounge offers excellent range of food and drink along with flight announcements. I was fortunate to fly the Qsuite on both legs of the journey. It offers so much privacy and the in demand dining is a bonus. You are made to feel special and to be honest its more like a first class experience. I used Al Maha services to transit it was literally a 10 min transfer from the airbridge to the lounge and when it was time to board I was collected and fast tracked to board my second

Due to bad weather, the Abu Dhabi–Doha flight was delayed and then cancelled, causing a missed connection to Zagreb. Weather disruptions happen — that is not my complaint. The issue was the lack of assistance from Qatar Airways. There was no support, no hotel, no transport, no guidance. I was left to handle everything on my own for more than 24 hours at the airport. I am a Qatar Airways Gold member, and this experience was deeply disappointing.

This was an incredible experience. We always make use of Qatar airways but this time I decided to write a review! Very clean, neat, professional, friendly, humble. I had no problem or complaint. The absolute perfect ending to our European Holiday!

Qatar Airways is currently my go-to airline for long travel and that’s for a good reason. The extra legroom seat was super comfortable and the food was very very delicious. The infotainment had an up to date library and the screens were huge. The complimentary Starlink Wifi was amazing and reliable.

Review Title: ate and drank all that I wanted
Review: Flew Qatar from Casablanca to Doha on Xmas eve 2025. This was the 2nd leg oof three flights across Africa with QR and partners. A350-900, on time. Was checked in earlier. Came directly from hotel to gate upon immigration and security. Boarding clear but tedious as flight was completely full. Onboard FAs were busy but managed well the full flight and did not run out of any foods or drinks despite the flight were for 7 hours I ate and drank all that I wanted. Even French sparkling wine was given ad libitum and people appeared to consume a lot on a Christmas eve. Felt in love with their fish dish, purely wild catch. Two other hot dishes were offered. IFE functioned fully; easy to navigate. In Doha I was given a 4-star hotel paid by carrier as next flight (with Rwandair) was 8 hours ahead. This, despite that I had lost my status in their frequent flyer program and was unrecognised. Luggage transfers went smooth. Miles given instantly.

Crew was hard working again on both legs. HAN - DOH: My snack, chicken sandwich or calzone was bad. My meal, fried rice with shrimp, was bad, almost no shrimp inside,only chopped pieces) DOH -DUS: We were bussed to the plane which took very long. Food was not as announced on the app but was good, butter and salt provided on inquiry.

Review Title: revelation on this flight was Starlink
Review: Doha to Goa in Business Class on the 787-8. This aircraft carried Qatar Airways’ older pod-style Business Class seat. While not a Qsuite, it remains exceptionally comfortable. Onboard service, long a Qatar hallmark, was superb once again. Taken as a whole, this Business Class product sits at the pinnacle of commercial aviation, only a shade below the First Class offerings still found on select airlines. The revelation on this flight was Starlink. With fast, reliable Wi-Fi, the distinction between ground and inflight connectivity has all but vanished. A new era has begun.

Review Title: at the top of the industry
Review: Copenhagen to Doha in Business Class on the 787-8. Qatar Airways fielded the older pod-style Business Class seat on this aircraft - short of the Qsuite experience, but still exceptionally comfortable. Service was warm, attentive, and efficient throughout. At this point, Qatar’s Business Class feels like a mature, confidently delivered product, and it is easy to understand why the airline remains at the top of the industry. The sole disappointment was connectivity: this aircraft was not fitted with Starlink, and the paid Wi-Fi was mediocre. Where it matters, though - punctuality and comfort - Qatar continues to set the standard.

I recently flew with Qatar Airways and had an excellent experience from start to finish. From check-in to arrival, everything felt well-organized and professional. Qatar remains my choice of airline for my home travels as due to convenience, less stops, better service and experience. I flew American from Dallas to Doha and had to pay for my second baggage even as an international passenger and will have to pay for return ticket too. I would request Qatar Airways to advocate for second baggage free when partnering customers who have to fly American and Qatar. That would be a great service to passengers. Boarding was smooth, and the flight itself was calm and well-managed. Small details — like frequent check-ins from the crew and overall comfort — really stood out and made the journey enjoyable. Everyone at Qatar was courteous. I love the airport assistance that is available throughout and would like to send kudos for managing it so efficiently. Overall, Qatar Airways delivered a high-quality travel experience, and I would confidently choose them again and recommend them to others.

My experience was quite underwhelming, the segments I took were delayed minimum 1 hour. Forget about oneworld alliance status to access priority boarding or any of the perks partner airlines traditionally give, with Qatar I needed to constantly ask them and they don’t seem to like reciprocating the entitlements. The plane from Melbourne to Doha had seen better days, the cabin was uncomfortable and tired, the toilets were filthy.

As a frequent traveler and a loyal Privilege Club member, I choose Qatar Airways for the exceptional level of service they provide. From the moment of check-in to arrival, the experience is seamless and well-organized. The cabin crew are highly professional, attentive, and focused on passenger comfort. The on-board product reflects premium quality, with excellent seating comfort, in-flight entertainment, and high standards of hospitality. Hamad International Airport continues to set a global benchmark for efficiency and passenger experience. Qatar Airways’ commitment to service excellence is evident in every journey. Qatar Airways remains my airline of choice, and I highly recommend them to anyone seeking reliability, comfort, and world-class service.

The experience felt consistently refined and genuinely human. Qatar Airways delivers a level of customer service that is calm, professional, and warm — never forced, always attentive. The cabin comfort, quality of materials, and overall fit and finish of the aircraft clearly reflect a strong attention to detail. Everything feels intentional and well maintained, creating a sense of ease throughout the journey. A special highlight was the vegan special meal. It was thoughtfully prepared, flavorful, and clearly designed with care rather than treated as an afterthought. As someone who pays close attention to service details, I can say that while there is always room for small refinements in any premium operation, the overall execution was excellent and memorable.
Route: Sao Paulo to Moscow via Doha

Review: Qatar Airways lost my bag and I finally received the bag at my destination in Muscat after 3 days. They requested me to submit several documents that I complied with. It has been over 3 months, no compensation has been issued to me. I have spent countless hours trying to resolve the issue. I have called their customer service call centers many times. I have sent dozens of emails. Nothing has worked so far.

Small, mediocre meal, no salt and butter provided. On a 6 hour flight only one meal, no pre-landing snack. Wine served in small doses in plastic sparkling wine glasses. No amenity kit. Other than that, the crew was hard working Seats in Boeing 787 have a weird de-reclining behaviour, sometimes making the crew having to push the seats

On Oct 14, 2025, I was scheduled to fly from DOH to BOS with Qatar in business class with my mom. However, I had this extreme pain in my stomach (I later found out to be kidney stone) and during checkin I was told I could not fly as I had to head to the hospital. Since I was in a foreign country, I did not want any major procedures or imaging due to insurance coverage. About 4 hours later, I was discharged from the hospital and headed back to Doha airport to reschedule my alternative flight as my original flight has already taken off. Since I booked the flight using partner airline miles, Qatar told me they could not do anything and instead I need to speak with the partner. Ultimately I did manage to reach the partner airline through online chat and they told me I need to cancel and rebook but since it was a no show since I missed the flight there is nothing they can so. Thinking through even if I was able to cancel, there is absolutely no way I can book last minute tickets using the original miles.  I explained to the Qatar manager my chat with the partner airline and he still said there is nothing they can do but then stepped away to discuss the matter with other employees. After 10 minutes he came back and said your health is the most important thing, just let us know when you would like to fly, tomorrow or any time we will take care of you. urned out there were 2 business class seats available for the following day and we were able to get on. Qatar did not need to do this but they did and I am completely grateful of the gesture and customer service. For those of you who have flown business with Qatar out of Doha know the business class check in area and the employees there willing to help. I’m not sure if I would have gotten the same level of service in economy but I am forever grateful.

Review Title: give no butter or salt and pepper
Review: The only plus was that it had Starlink wifi. Other than that, food came out far less than other airlines, large breaks in-between food services, kids were starving on way home from doha to Brisbane leg. They give no butter or salt and pepper as a standard with food. Having four flights with Qatar on the round trip, each plane staff enforced different rules for the baby bassinet, one staff refused to let child use at all saying he was too big.  They have no courtesy baby strollers at the airport including Doha airport.

The Q-Suites hard product was excellent—comfortable seating, strong privacy, and a great screen (9/10). Unfortunately my food was largely inedible (dry, overcooked chicken and stale bread), and meal service was confusing, with my presumed lunch entrée served at breakfast and no option to change. Service was surprisingly inattentive, and I had to request basic items like bedding. No additional meal was offered to me on a 15-hour flight. The service lacked the proactive care I expected.

Review Title: a very comfortable way to travel
Review: Excellent return flights in Qsuites. Super comfortable, spacious, and enjoyable. Food, drink, and service was of a consistently high standard, though I find the crew are really at their best on the shorter flights than the longhaul JFK-DOH flight - they tend to check out a bit and service can be scratchy. Many crew seem to disappear, leaving just a couple of crew to deal with the entire business class. Due to the dine-on-demand system, the crew were often short-staffed and it could take some time to get service. But overall a very comfortable way to travel. Excellent IFE.
Route: New York to Nairobi via Doha

Review: All fights on time. Planes clean, seats comfortable, food and service good. No complaints really, although it seems the menus offer a plethora of items which aren't always available. Found some surprising music gems in their inflight entertainment system. Could not get the wifi to work. I did not have time to check out their lounge in Doha due to the short connection time but the lounge in BKK is quite the comfortable little palace. Really impressed with their ground staff in Doha - absolutely the nicest and most professional people I have come across in my over 50 decades of flying.

The flight was initially delayed by four hours, which can happen but there was no communication from the airline. Because of this delay, we missed our connection and had to book a new one. They offered us a transfer 24 hours later, and refused to provide hotel accommodation, meaning our only option was to sleep on the airport floor. Lounge access was denied. We received a $20 voucher for food and drinks, which is nowhere near sufficient to cover 24 hours at an airport.

Excellent flights. Comfortable aircraft, very good food and drink, and outstanding cabin crew. Smooth transfer at Doha, impressive airport with a “jungle” in the centre. The only disappointment was the lounge at Johannesburg. The Menzie lounge was very ordinary, in contrast with superb one at Doha

Cabin service personable, professional, attentive, efficient, welcoming, exceptionally gentle and polite; excellent dress. The 'perfect package" that inspired admiration for excellent service over a very long shift (13+ hours); very high praise earned for a difficult job well done. Noisy PA gong sound accompanied by illuminated blue ceiling light; random and repeated to a level of high annoyance; wakens passengers trying to rest or sleep. PA communication in English excessively loud and fast; impossible to understand for an English speaker. Seat space very cramped, very uncomfortable; seats have hard arm rests. My food was not always hot; satisfactory choice; bread roll without butter; poor cuisine selection; better wine quality would be appreciated. Qatar Airways scores very well on customer service but abysmally on passenger comfort. However, for someone who is determined to travel to the other side of the world, they deliver value for money.

I had unfortunately severe illness on my flight (QR843 and QR071 on 21.11.25) and needed to check in more. Despite this very unexpected situation, the board staff has been incredibly polite and impressive towards me. They were both friendly, warm and have got everything done to make me feel as comfortable as possible. The service was overwhelming – from fresh towels, water and medicines and helpful words to discreet but constant communication. Despite my physically very bad situation I felt safe and well cared for thanks to the exceptionally good staff. A big thank you to the entire team on board! Ich hatte auf meinem Flug (QR843 und QR071 am 21.11.25) leider starke Übelkeit und musste mich mehrfach übergeben. Trotz dieser sehr unangenehmen Situation hat sich das Bordpersonal unglaublich fürsorglich und aufmerksam um mich gekümmert. Sie waren jederzeit freundlich, geduldig und haben alles getan, damit ich mich so wohl wie möglich fühle. Der Service war hervorragend – von frischen Tüchern,Wasser und Medikamenten und beruhigenden Worten bis hin zur diskreten, aber konstanten Aufmerksamkeit. Trotz meines körperlich sehr schlechten Zustands habe ich mich dank des außergewöhnlich guten Personals sicher und gut aufgehoben gefühlt. Ein großes Dankeschön an das gesamte Team an Bord!

Seven year old Boeing 777 in OK condition. The  meal options were poor for me and I skipped the dinner and breakfast. The Starlink WiFi service was excellent. I found the internal lighting was poor I had difficulty reading. IFE was a mixed bag, movies were ok tv and games a disaster

What a delightful experience flying Qatar Airways for the first time. Both outbound and inbound legs were on A350-1000 with Q-Suites. Configuration is 1-2-1 with lie flat beds. Separate check in for business class passengers was very welcome. The Q-Suites offer total privacy. Service is impeccable. A welcome cold drink, Arabic coffee, Qatari dates and refreshing towel. Amenities included noise cancelling headset and pajamas. The letter were very soft and comfortable. Dining is par excellence. Angus Beef tenderloin, key lime pie with citrus meringue and assorted bakery rolls. Appetizer consisted of Qatari mezze with pita bread. The IFE options were very good with movies, tv shows, music and live sports. Surprisingly, no live news channels. Ground crew at check in and boarding were all very friendly and welcoming. In flight crew provided excellent service. WiFi via Starlink connected seamlessly and blazing fast from gate to gate.

Review Title: Lots of kudos to Qatar Airways
Review: Lots of kudos to Qatar Airways. Because of an airport strike in Rome, my connecting flight to Rome with another airline made the link to the Qatar Airways flight uncertain. I contacted QATAR online to see if I could change my flight to Adelaide so it started in Athens rather than Rome. I was communicating with a real person in real time. This is much better than having to ring a phone where language becomes an issue as does getting through. This can be difficult or impossible when you are overseas. My Qatar Airways  online agent helped me work it out and the booking was changed. All discussions with airlines should be this easy. There was a service fee but it was reasonable in the circumstances. The flight was excellent. Great staff boarding and during the flight. Friendly and professional. We enjoyed the meals and drinks. Better quality wines than we expected in economy. The accompanying salad was surprisingly fresh,- don't know how they do that. And we received window seats with an empty seat in the middle for the DOHA-Adelaide leg.

Outbound leg was on a former Oman Air Boeing 787-8with 2x2x2 seating. With no gate available, we were transported from Al Safwa Lounge by bus to the airplane. Had to climb the stairs to board the aircraft which is difficult with carryon bags. There was no Starlink WiFi on this 90 mins flight. Flight crew was very courteous and professional. Dining was par excellence with excellent taps sized snack meals given short flight (lamb biryani and vegetable ratatouille with mashed potatoes). Welcome drink of Arabic coffee flavored with cardamom and Qatari dates plus the refreshing towel. Inbound leg was on Airbus A330-300 with 2x2x2 seating featuring 17 inch TV screens. Service was excellent. We were welcomed with the traditional Arabic coffee and dates. Coffee served on fine china. The overhead cabins seemed narrow unlike the 787s. On arrival in DOH, we again had to board a bus to the terminal.
I love the service by Qatar Airways. The staff are always friendly and very polite to me.

I use Qatar airways because of their excellent service. I use economy class as i cannot afford business class and first class and they’re so good in customer service. On this particular trip I was upgraded to first class on the Riyadh to Doha, this really validated why I recommend Qatar Airways to my colleagues.
It was a great experience. Super friendly staff and amazing onboard entertainment. The food was delicious. We boarded on time and the plane took off also on time.

Gold membership is useless, in Doha they let economy board first, then the priority line is opened and I ended up getting on the flight last behind economy, with all storage space used. A seat screen was broken all flight, keep reseting. Staff don't understand as I l said vegetarian, they give me chicken. Staff was rude to me. The lounge food in Singapore is awful; better eat in foodcourt.

Wonderful stay at Al Safwa lounge. Unfortunately a bus gate, but this time well organized. Well maintained Qsuite. Service and food outstanding. This was the best flight I had with Qatar for a long time. The staff was motivated, efficient and was still able to have a friendly chat. WiFi lightning fast throughout the flight. Flight arrived 20 minutes early at Changi airport. I wish all Qatar flights were that good.

Check in friendly, efficient and smooth. Priority security an  unorganised mess but this is not something the airline can control. Contrast lounge had a long queue of guests trying to get in. They were allowed in only in batches since "we don’t enough staff to wash the plates and cutlery." I left after a drink since it was almost time to board as I’ve lost so much time by then. Boarding organized. Seat comfortable and private. Service good. Food was ok, but nothing special. I miss the days when there was always one meal with German specialties while flying ex Germany. Flight arrived ahead of time.

Excellent airline. I'm a big bloke (203cm) and need to sit at the emergency exit for the legroom. While trying to book this, a small app glitch prevented payment from being finalised, but their agent on live chat was able to assist and finalise this within a few minutes. Important to note it was a real person and not a bot. Everyone jokes about airline food, but their vegetarian meal was so good I asked for seconds! Hamad International Airport is massive but so well organised it was a breeze to get through.

I recently traveled with Qatar Airways from Medina to Amsterdam via Doha on October 16th, returning the following day. The Medina–Doha flights were operated by an all-economy A321. he absence of a business class option on this route removes both the comfort of traveling in business class and access to the Al Mourjan or Al Safwa lounges in Doha. The seats on this aircraft felt significantly tighter, with smaller legroom, and the cabin felt cramped and uncomfortable. Although my Gold Privilege Club membership granted me access to the Gold Lounge, it was clearly not comparable to the premium lounges that business class passengers enjoy.

The cabin power failed 2 hours into the flight and it was announced the power should be restored soon; which never happened until getting to the gate in Doha. The cockpit never addressed the issue. The power failure resulted in no lights, I had to eat with the mobile phone torch lights on and the IFE did not work. My seat could not be adjusted and had to be put into an upright position upon arrival manually. Upon arrival in Doha, I had already an email from Qatar stating that due to the seat failure (the other issues were never mentioned) I would receive as a gesture of good will a compensation of 9000 Avios.  That offer is an insult.

The flight and service was excellent, BUT!!! The baggage service was very bad. One of my luggage with fragile contents were damaged. and no compensation. Explanation: "fragile items are transported with utmost care however our liability for such items are limited."

On the day of our flight from Athens there was a public transport strike and we had great difficulty getting to the airport. After receiving a call from Qatar ground staff at Athens airport enquiring where we were, as bag drop was about to close, we explained our situation and quickly received instructions on who to see upon our late arrival at the airport. Qatar staff fast tracked our check in and then escorted us quickly through security and immigration so that we just made the flight. Cannot imagine another airline would go to the lengths Qatar staff did to help us and will be forever grateful in such a stressful situation.

Service and F&B at Al Safwa lounge good. Boarding at contact gate well organised. IFE did not work. A technician was called to fix it since BC was full and no other seat could be offered. He was unable to fix the issue and IFE unusable and the WiFi which did not work either. Service and F&B were decent. Flight arrived on time. Feedback in regard to the non functioning IFE remains unanswered.

Munich to Doha, an exceptional experience from start to finish. The cabin crew were professional, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. The seat comfort, inflight dining, and entertainment were all outstanding—true to Qatar Airways’ five-star reputation. The flight was punctual and smooth, making the journey both relaxing and enjoyable. Highly recommended.

Review Title: I requested this to be refunded
Review: We had a flight cancelled which resulted in us spending hours trying to reschedule a new flight, not to mention the extra costs involved with additional accommodation and connecting transport changes. On the morning we were due to fly, we did not receive any notification that the flight had been cancelled and I discovered the cancellation through the app. After arriving home from our trip, I emailed in regards to the extra costs we incurred and as we had paid for seat selection (which we didn't receive) I requested this to be refunded. It is now 10 weeks later, 6 emails later and still no reply whatsoever from Qatar. Their customer service is non existent.

Review Title: a bus ride from the terminal
Review: The flight is in September which is still pretty hot in Qatar and it was a late morning departure, the plane was parked a bus ride from the terminal, the bus ride was hot. The plane was a very old ex Oman Air A330-300 and was showing it’s age, the toilets were tiny and nowhere near the quality of Qatar Airways A320s. I will continue to use Qatar Airways as I know their standards but on this route I will stick to RJ. I am a Platinum Member on since 2017 I was not recognized and overlooked this staff needs a lot more experience. Alsafwa lounge at Doha is the best lounge I have seen the spa has an amazing reflexology massage service.

I travel to Africa a few times a year and always choose Qatar Airways because of the excellent service you consistently provide. On my recent flight from Jeddah to Doha on 23 September 2025 (Flight QR1185), I was once again very impressed by your cabin crew. In particular, I would like to commend Ceren, who was extremely professional, kind, and caring. She served passengers with genuine warmth, lots of smiles, and a clear willingness to go the extra mile to ensure everyone was comfortable. Her dedication and attitude reflect the very best of Qatar Airways, and I believe she will have an excellent career with your company. Thank you once again for another great journey, and please extend my gratitude to Ceren and the rest of the team.

Contact lounge at FRA airport crowded and poor choice of breakfast items. Boarding was well organized. Both flights in Mini Qsuite which I prefer over th Qsuite. Both flights on time and service was good. Al Safwa lounge in Doha good as usual. The big letdown was the food. Presentation and taste was not what I am used to and the quality of wine was reduced. WiFi not working, transfer gate system through the entertainment syste not working.

My recent flights with Qatar helped remind me of what makes a truly world class airline. Cabin crew truly SERVE the passenger - every single passenger every single time. I watched it happen consistently across two flights over 16 hours with multiple crew and multiple passengers. No request is too much of an issue. I had the most incredible creamy chicken curry (in economy) that was better than any business class dish I have had; the entertainment selection is brilliant and getting better; and the baggage allowance to Africa flights (2 23kg) keeps them with all the other competition. There was a medical emergency on the flight to London and they handled it as a team like consummate professionals. Combined with the incredible experience transiting through the lush faux rain forest of Hamad, Doha, Qatar is surely the best option. One (big) bugbear is the charging of seats even at online check-in - its cheap, miserly and unnecessary and none of the African carriers (Rwandair; Ethiopian or Kenyan) on this route do this - but Emirates does so I guess it's who they see as their competition. All else considered - Qatar well done - your crew, onboard overall service and transfer is world class.
Route: London Heathrow to Lusaka via Doha

Review: Al Safwa F lounge in Doha excellent as always. Boarding not well organised. B789 mini Qsuite comfortable and private. Food was decent. Service was efficient and friendly. Arrival 20 minutes ahead of schedule

Review Title: Mini Qsuite very private and comfortable
Review: Check in fast and friendly, contract Miracle lounge with poor breakfast offerings. Boarding rather unorganised. Mini Qsuite on the 789 very private and comfortable. Service friendly and efficient. WiFi did not work. My food was not very tasty and poor in presentation. The usually top notch Thai dessert ex BKK was coconut ice-cream (with lots of ice crystals) and two half bananas. Connecting gates system through the entertainment system did not work. Arrived on time. Overall an ok flight with poor quality lounge and on board food.

We choose our seats when booking, and they changed to 32 for Islamabad-Doha) which was middle of the airplane. I asked the person checking in, he says there’s nothing he can do as aircraft was changed and seats have been reassigned. Fortunately we got the bulkhead seats we had selected for the flight Doha-Boston.

Initially, I was supposed to be traveling with Qatar Airways' partner, but due to unfortunate circumstances, I was rebooked to an earlier trip the next day with inconvenience compensation. Qatar Airways' service has always been exceptional! I have been a loyal client. The flight I took on 31/8/25 was fantastic and extremely comfortable. Thanks to Vanessa, the flight attendant who attended me while on the trip.

Review Title: I will remain a loyal customer
Review: I want to sincerely thank Qatar Airways for their incredible kindness during a very difficult moment. While checking in at Jeddah Airport, I couldn’t afford the extra baggage fees. The supervisor and assistant at the counter showed me compassion I’ll never forget — they allowed me the extra baggage allowance without charging me anything.  This experience showed me that Qatar Airways is not only a world-class airline but also one that truly cares about its passengers beyond business. Because of this, I will remain a loyal customer.

Boarding was efficient, friendly, personable welcome by cabin crew. Preflight drink offered quickly, guidance given on screen and seat operation. Meal orders taken (dine on demand). What a comfy seat that turned into one of the best lie flat beds I've had in the sky. The attentive service from our dedicated crew member was outstanding. The quality and quantity of the food and beverages were well beyond my expectations. The luxurious in-flight bar and lounge was, well, luxurious and a wonderful place to meet fellow travellers. Naturally the mandatory photo standing behind the bar was necessary. For a 15 hour non-stop flight, I was more than impressed with this Airline. No need for a Q-Suite, the A380 business class seat is far better in my view. Well done Qatar Airways, I will definitely fly QR again.

When booking, the flight wa shown as a Qsuite Boeing 777. Less than 24 hours before departure, the aircraft was swapped to an outdated 2-2-2 Business Class, with no privacy and no direct aisle access. At that point, rebooking was impossible. Customer Care has been unresponsive: after multiple attempts I received no reply.

Review Title: causing me to miss my flight
Review: I was scheduled to travel from Johannesburg (JHB) to Doha (DOH) and onward to Vienna (VIE) on 14 August 2025. Upon arrival at the check-in counter, three different agents attempted to process my check-in but appeared uncertain, repeatedly consulting a Team Leader. Despite having a visa, valid ticket, and all required supporting documents, I was eventually asked to stand aside. As delays continued, the manager instructed the ground staff to close the gate, ultimately causing me to miss my flight. This delay and lack of proper handling were due to the inefficiency of the ground staff. Following this incident, I approached KLM to book an alternative route. Their staff reviewed my documents and could not understand why Qatar Airways had denied me boarding, as my documentation was in order. I returned to the Qatar Airways counter later that day. At this point, the staff had changed, and I was asked to present my documents again. This was the first time that a staff member took photographs of my documents and sent them to Vienna for verification. The duty manager confirmed there was nothing wrong with my documents and stated that I could board provided I contacted the call centre to amend my ticket. I spoke with four different agents, but calls repeatedly dropped, and none of the agents who promised to call back actually did. The final call centre representative I spoke to quoted ZAR 17,000 to rebook my ticket. I do not accept that I should be liable for this cost. I have since boarded a Kenya Airways flight to Vienna via Nairobi and Amsterdam without a complication or request for additional documents. This demonstrates that my paperwork was correct and the initial refusal by Qatar Airways was unjustified.

Review Title: an experience that borders on First
Review: Doha to Copenhagen in Business Class. There is a reason Qatar Airways remains at the top: exceptional comfort in both hard and soft product. This aircraft did not feature the storied Qsuite but the older configuration, which is still among the best in the sky. Service was warm, polished, and attentive. The WiFi was patchy, though with Starlink integration on the horizon one hopes this will soon be resolved. In sum, no airline delivers greater comfort in Business Class. Qatar offers an experience that borders on First.

Goa (Mopa airport) to Doha in Business Class A flawless flight. Boarding was smooth and the on-board service impeccable. This aircraft did not feature the celebrated Qsuite, but the Business Class configuration was still extremely comfortable. My only quibble: the paid WiFi was virtually unusable, dropping out with frustrating regularity.

On August 6th, we flew an economy class connecting flight via Doha from Warsaw to the Seychelles via Doha. The first flight, an A330, had comfortable seats and ample legroom. The Doha stopover included a half-hour bus ride at night. The second flight, a Boeing 777, had much less legroom and narrower seats. Also, the cabin temperature was freezing, with a vent pushing cold air onto your faces and shoulders.

I recently traveled with Qatar Airways from Colombo, Sri Lanka to London Heathrow, and it was an outstanding experience from start to finish. The first leg of my journey, from Colombo to Doha, was on a Boeing 787, followed by a Boeing 777-300 from Doha to London. Both aircraft were spotless, modern, and departed  on time. MThe cabin crew were warm, attentive, and caring. I felt personally looked after throughout the flight. The food served on board was excellent, with a variety of options. The inflight entertainment system was equally impressive, offering a wide range of movies, music, and programs. Qatar Airways provided a premium travel experience that I would happily recommend to others.

Cape Town to Doha my inflight entertainment wasn't working, I could use free wifi. My return flight from Doha to Cape Town it was not working again. After a long layover in Doha fron Bangkok I wasn't in the mood to be on my phone so nothing to watch. After asking the crew twice to check, no inflight entertainment. Bad!!
Route: Bangkok to Cape Town via Doha

Review: This is the best airline I’ve experienced as economy class, and I love the wide range of options of entertainment. Even though we had medium space for legs, they give you a bag on long distance flights) with compression socks, shades for your eyes, ear plugs and a toothbrush /  toothpaste. That kind of hospitality definitely makes them deserve 9th titled best airline!  The ground staff also organized the groups punctually and kindly. Free WiFi!

Flying Business Class with Qatar Airways was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment I boarded, I was impressed by the attention to detail and the warm welcome from the cabin crew. They were genuinely helpful, attentive without being intrusive, and went out of their way to ensure I was comfortable throughout the flight. What really made the experience memorable was the professionalism and kindness of the crew. Whether it was offering assistance with the seat settings, adjusting lighting, or just checking in regularly with a smile, their service felt personal and sincere. Qatar airways has been my regular airline whenever I fly.

Review Title: very disappointed in the qualty of service
Review: Having arrived in Doha at 6.00am with my family (including my wife and two small children) from Melbourne, we were due to have a 3h 30m stopover in Doha before boarding a flight to Venice. The scheduled flight for 9.30am was delayed, with a new departure time set for 13.00. Great experience for our young sleep deprived kids (4yo & 2yo). Upon boarding the delayed flight and having commenced taxiing the runway, the captain advised that due to a technical issue, we were required to return to the gate to await further engineer inspection. Having waited almost two hours for further information and grappling with our young children who were losing it by now, we were then advised that we must disembark the plane and return to the terminal, with a revised scheduled departure time of 18.00. This revised departure time meant that we would land in Venice at 22.20, which left us unable to make a connecting train to Trieste where we had accommodation booked for the evening. I had to book emergency accommodation in Venice for 1 night and forego the booked accommodation in Trieste. On the delayed flight, they even charged me to use the Wifi so that I could confirm our accommodation - typically an experience expected on a budget low cost airline. We did not arrive to our accommodation in Venice until after midnight and were almost left stranded as we couldn't communicate with the host who was about to depart from the accom. We lost a day of our holiday due to this delay, along with the incurred expense of booking emergency accommodation and loss of booked accommodation, due to the 8h 30m delay at Doha airport. I'm very disappointed in the qualty of service and the mental impact this had on my family at the start of a dream holiday. Indeed, an awful experience to have to endure with no word of apology or possible compensation from Qatar Airways. A bang average airline dressed up as the best in the world. Laughable. Will never book with them again.

Review Title: forgotten how good it can be
Review: It's been a while since I flew Qatar Airways and I had forgotten how good it can be. Lounge at Heathrow provided a fabulous a la carte lunch, and the onboard dining was great for airline meals. Staff were very attentive and polite, and the Qsuite is unbeatable. I've used Emirates and BA on some recent trips and they're simply not close to Qatar. 5 Star at its best.

My journey on flight QR039 from Doha to Paris was a perfect blend of comfort, service, and efficiency. The check-in process at Hamad International Airport was smooth and well-organized. Friendly staff assisted with swift baggage drop and clear instructions. Boarding was timely and stress-free, with dedicated lanes for different classes and priority passengers. The aircraft was modern and immaculately clean. The seats offered excellent legroom and ergonomics, even in Economy. Adjustable headrests, a plush seat cushion, and a thoughtfully designed tray table added to the in-flight ease. The in-flight meals were restaurant-quality.  Drinks were served frequently and with a smile, and special meal requests were handled flawlessly.  Qatar’s Oryx One system remains one of the best. It had a wide range of new-release movies, documentaries, music, and games. The touch screen was responsive, and the interface was easy to navigate. Onboard Wi-Fi was available and worked well for light browsing and messaging. The cabin crew on QR039 were attentive and professional. From helping with bags to offering water before being asked, their service was personal and polished. The flight departed on time and landed at Paris Charles de Gaulle smoothly. Disembarkation was quick, and baggage arrived without delay, rounding off a flawless travel experience.

Review Title: service was both warm and precise
Review: Copenhagen to Doha on Qatar Airways Business Class (B787-8). This flight did not feature the Qsuite layout, but the standard Business Class seating was extremely comfortable. Qatar’s cabin crew delivered service that was both warm and precise, among the best in the industry. Overall, the experience reflected a high standard. There were, however, a few shortcomings. The pilot’s announcement was difficult to understand, marred by poor audio quality. The WiFi connection was sluggish and unreliable, despite the $20 charge. As for the food, I chose the Indian vegetarian option, which arrived swimming in oil. A lighter, more balanced preparation would have been preferable.

Review Title: maintains a high standard of service
Review: Doha to Goa on Qatar Airways Business Class (B787-8). This flight also lacked the Qsuite configuration, but the conventional Business Class seating was more than comfortable for a short 3-hour hop. Qatar maintains a high standard of service, and the crew lived up to that reputation. The flight left Doha and reached Goa on schedule. The only letdown was the WiFi. I paid the full $20 and once again received only patchy service. One hopes this will be addressed once Starlink is introduced across the fleet.

On 23 June 2025, I flew from Hong Kong to Doha. The flight was diverted and arrived in Doha later than scheduled due to the Iranian attack on Qatar, and this caused a substantial delay for my trip. When I arrived in Doha, I learned that the connecting flight to Sao Paulo on 24th, QR779, was canceled.  I approached staff at the airport but he was not at all helpful. The only thing he could tell me was to go to the transfer counter. When I went to the transfer counter area, I witnessed pandemonium. I could not figure out how to line up for the transfer desk, and asked another staff and he told me to go to the business class lounge. I followed that advice and went there. A staff there told me to just wait for a few hours and come back because the company is handling the situation and things should go back to normal then. In the end, I had to spend 20 some hours before I can get a new ticket, and another 20-some hours at the airport before getting on the plane to Sao Paulo on 26th, two days later than the original connection flight. Meanwhile Qatar Airways put my luggage to an earlier flight to Sao Paulo, I had a tagging device to learn that my luggage was already in Sao Paulo for some time when I arrived there.
Route: Hong Kong to Sao Paulo via Doha

Review: Boarding at Doha airport was chaotic and unorganised, despite gate being manned by 6 agents. When lines started to form at boarding time, the supervisor screamed at the guests: "Sit down, the aircraft is still being cleaned; you need to be patient." Chaos followed when boarding finally started 35 min late. Economy started first which caused business class passengers to complain, which resulted in a rather messy boarding procedure. The service and food was the big letdown on this 6 hour night flight. Service completed almost 3 hours after takeoff which left 2 hours of rest before the lights went on again. The meal I received was a soggy stick mass of pasta with of tomato sauce. (They called it Penne Arabiata) There was no taste and an unpleasant texture. This was one of the worst inflight meal I ever had. The flight attendant in charge of my section was slow, unmotivated and robotic in her service rendered. The seats on the B789 were tight and uncomfortable, making it impossible for a good rest.

Qatar Airways cancelled the connecting flight on the departure date when I was at the airport to check in for the first flight. So I had to quickly get another flight with another airline at the airport to fly on the same day. They did not contact me to inform that they can only process a refund if I cancel the return flight as well, which I only found out a couple days after I contacted the airline to check on the partial refund for cancelling my departure flights.

Review Title: a good airline and decent value
Review: First trip using Qatar Airways. My lasting impression is that the cabin crew work extremely hard throughout the whole flights, unlike the crew on certain other airlines. The food in Economy was very poor though. The safety briefings are via pre-recorded videos featuring an actor who turns the whole issue of safety into a huge joke, which is really inappropriate. The pilots were superb and made incredibly smooth landings on all flights. Overall a good airline and decent value. That food though..oh dear!
Route: Bangkok to London Heathrow via Doha

Review: Flight from Bali to Doha arrived 8 mins early, flight from Doha to Vienna arrived 77 minutes late. 2 toilets for 130 passengers in economy on the A320. Although Wheel chair was requested I had to walk to the aircraft.

Review Title: pay attention to details that matter
Review: It is always a pleasure to experience Qatar's world class service. They are the best airline because they pay attention to details that matter to the customer. The cabin crew is efficient professionals. I like that they always know what they are doing, as if they take pride in what they are doing. They are also very warm and personable.  They chat with you rather than talk to you - if you understand what I mean. I also like that they remember your preferences OR take initiative when they need to. My cabin attendant remembered that I preferred sparkling water and delivered it with each meal, but he also knew with which of my meal courses to serve my two different wine selections. The food is excellent and the restrooms are kept clean. Pilots y communicate ahead of time if there will be turbulence or if any information needs to be shared. Little things like these make me feel like they care and if they care then I feel comfortable and safe.

Al Safwa lounge in Doha outstanding as always. Last minute aircraft switch from A350-1000 to B773, but was equipped with Qsuite. Holding area at Gate A7 did not provide seats for the fully booked flight. Flight departed 30 min later, which resulted in a 1h delay into Heathrow, because of the restricted airspace at the moment. Food was great. Caviar service was a nice touch. The service was ok, but lacked in consistency. Coffee was served 30 min before the meal arrived, and it also took up to 30 min to clear plate between courses during the meal. New WiFi lighting fast, which resulted in loud WiFi chats among some pax. I had to tell them to stop! I think this will become a big issue in the future and IFA should be firmer on those inconsiderate people. Overall a great flight with some room for improvement for onboard service

Check in at Sydney was quick efficient and very friendly staff. Boarding a little chaotic, it was a free for all and anyone used any Boarding lane. Eventually on board, very friendly and personalised greeting. Not long after settling in, pretake off drink offered, explanation of seat operation, menu and how dining on demand works. Not long after, meal orders were taken. Once airborne, the crew switched into their routine. It was faultless. It was consistent and again personable. The seating was comfortable. In lie flat position, I slept a solid 8 hours. All food was of a high standard, well presented and taste was fantastic. Beverages were also well presented and always available. The highlight for the wife and to a lesser extent me, was the inflight bar and lounge which was well patronised when we were there. The crew also engaging and very friendly and genuinely interested in their guests. Overall, we rate this flight tops. Way above some of the other major airlines we have flown and better than First Class on some... just saying. Consistency, personable and a very enjoyable first time experience flying with Qatar Airways

Review Title: Service hectic and not overly friendly
Review: Check in fast and friendly. Contract lounge with poor food selection and uncleared tables. B773 on this 4h red eye night flight. Boarding via remote stand. I was the first on board, but was ignored by the FA and had to ask for my welcome drink while the plane was already moving. Meal order was taken after take off, which isn’t ideal for a red eye flight. The new irritating safety video was shown with no sound. Service was hectic and not overly friendly. An orange light was left on throughout the night. Overall, a rather average flight. The same flight from Doha to ADD was in comparison so much better.

Al Safwa lounge great as always for my long layover. Boarding via gate rather unorganised. A320 with the flat bed seats. Food and drinks choices outstanding for the 4h flight. What made the flight special was the service rendered by the hardworking cabin crew. It’s not easy to provide a full service for an almost full cabin on a narrow body aircraft, but the two in the front cabin worked wonders; highly recommended!

Check in was friendly and efficient. Contract Miracle lounge at the new Satellite terminal quiet, with poor breakfast options. I should have used the other OW lounges like Cathay or JAL. It’s hard to comprehend why Qatar does not give access to their own lounge to QR Platinum members.  Flight was uneventful with average service and food. WiFi did not work most of the time, seats tight and hard. Flight arrived 15 minutes ahead of schedule
